creator: wjw@digiware.nl
text:
(In reply to Jan Beich from comment #11)

Hi Jan,

A regular ceph build in jenkinst would look a like:
http://cephdev.digiware.nl:8180/jenkins/job/ceph-master/lastBuild/console

and if you there search for boost_thread you'll see that in that build the libraries are bound in a totally different way:
    /usr/local/lib/libboost_thread.so

So I'm not why all of a sudden Cmake want to transform this into -lBoost::thread.
Where I assume that Cmake expects Clang to find the correct lib with this option?

I certainly do not follow the development of CMake as it is, and guess I'll have to look into this. But I'm rather busy ATM.

Is Ceph the only one giving you such a hard time?
